data.title: Unify testing philosophy: instruction-file boundary rule and shared standard consolidation
data.description:
## Context

When implementation-plan generates a plan for stories that modify LLM instruction files (skills, prompts, agent definitions, protocols), the TDD plan reviewer demands behavioral tests that can't exist for Markdown artifacts, and implementation-plan suggests grep-based content checks as a workaround. These grep checks are change-detector tests — the exact anti-pattern the behavioral testing standard was built to eliminate. This causes plan review cycles to deadlock on every story that touches instruction files — affecting any developer or sprint orchestrator running implementation-plan on skill/prompt/agent stories.

## Success Criteria

- The shared behavioral testing standard contains a rule defining the testing boundary for non-executable artifacts: test only at the deterministic integration interface (contract schema validation, referential integrity, shim compliance, syntax checks, deployment prerequisites like test -x); do NOT test behavioral correctness of LLM instruction content
- The shared behavioral testing standard contains the acceptable structural test categories and explicitly prohibits test -f existence checks and grep-based content assertions as change-detector tests
- Implementation-plan's Behavioral Test Requirement section is replaced with a reference to the shared standard; the parallel rule set is eliminated
- Implementation-plan's test approach requirement uses Given/When/Then framing per the shared standard's Rule 2
- The TDD plan reviewer references the shared standard and scores instruction-file tasks against the boundary rule — accepting deterministic interface tests without demanding behavioral correctness assertions
- Agents that already reference the standard by path (red-test-writer, test-quality-overlay, red-test-evaluator) automatically consume the new rule — verified by read-only path-reference confirmation, no modifications to agent definition files
- After deployment, re-running /dso:implementation-plan on the paused instruction-file story 8163-83a4 produces a plan that passes TDD plan review without deadlocking on behavioral assertion demands — verified within the sprint session as a smoke test

## Scenario Analysis

- Structural test categories conflict with test-quality gate (critical, runtime): The standard's structural test categories must not contradict the pre-commit test quality gate's anti-pattern rules
- Partial file update causes inconsistent rule sets (high, runtime): Standard and SKILL.md must be updated atomically in the same commit
- Plan reviewer fails after section heading removal (high, deployment): Replacing Behavioral Test Requirement section could break parsers expecting that heading
- In-progress stories see inconsistent requirements (high, deployment): Stories planned under old rules may see redundant test flags after update
- Split-artifact stories have no resolution protocol (high, configuration): OUT OF SCOPE — implementation-plan's per-task testing_mode already separates instruction-file tasks from executable-code tasks

## Dependencies

8df3-61d0 (closed) — Behavioral testing standard and agent prompt reform

## Approach

Extend behavioral-testing-standard.md with a new rule defining the testing boundary for non-executable artifacts. Move acceptable structural test categories from implementation-plan into the standard; explicitly prohibit test -f and grep-based content assertions. Replace implementation-plan's Behavioral Test Requirement section with a reference to the standard. Reframe test approach requirement to Given/When/Then. Update TDD plan reviewer to reference the standard. Keep planning-specific items (unit exemption criteria, integration test rule, filename conventions) in implementation-plan as workflow orchestration rules. Split-artifact stories are out of scope — implementation-plan's per-task testing_mode already separates instruction-file tasks from executable-code tasks within a single story.
